content.full_text:
City of
Encinitas

May 22, 1992

Maura Wiegand
Mayor

Mr. Scott Halibert
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

John Davis
Deputy Mayor

Dear Sir:

I spoke to Mr. John Wallace today and he suggested that
I write for a legal opinion as to our Deputy Mayor's
ability to discuss and take action on the following
issue:

Anne Omsted
Council Member

There currently exists a traffic barrier located at the
end of Crest Drive, a residential road in the City of
Encinitas. Our City Council is considering removing that
barrier. Deputy Mayor Davis's home is located on Lahoud
Drive, 1 1/4 mile from the barrier. Lake Drive is a
circulation road and 100 feet from his home. (see
map)

Pamela Slater
Council Member

Our Council will be discussing this issue on June 10th
and would greatly appreciate a response prior to that
date.

Thank you in advance for any assistance you can provide.

Sincerely,

E. Gail Hano
Council Member

Deborah Cervone
Deputy City Clerk

Warren H Shafer
City Manager

James F. Benson
Assistant City Manager

527 Encinitas Boulevard, Encinitas, California 92024    TEL 619-944-5050 / FAX 619-632-9836    recycled paper

Crest Drive Review                    21                    Austin-Fousi Associates, Inc.

Figure 8
TRAVEL TIME ANALYSIS ROUTES

LEGEND
─────── Route 1
·········· Route 2
▲▲▲▲▲▲▲ Route 3

[The map shows various streets and locations including:]
- PACIFIC OCEAN
- Main Street
- I-5
- SANTA FE
- ENCINITAS
- RANCHO SANTA FE
- WINCHESTER
- [Various dotted and dashed lines indicating different routes]
- [Blue arrows and labels indicating "Approved layout" and other directional markers]

6-31

The image appears to be a mostly blank white page with only a few small marks or spots visible. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe.

embedding.qa_text: May 22, 1992 Mr. Scott Halibert General Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Dear Sir: I spoke to Mr. John Wallace today and he suggested that I write for a legal opinion as to our Deputy Mayor’s ability to discuss and take action on the following issue: There currently exists a traffic barrier located at the end of Crest Drive; a residential road in the City of Encinitas. Our City Council is considering removing that barrier. Deputy Mayor Davis’s home is located on Lahoud Drive, 1 1/4 mile from the barrier. Lake Drive is a circulation element road and 100 feet from his home. (see map) Our Council will be discussing this issue on June 10th and would greatly appreciate a response prior to that date. Thank you in advance for any assistance you can provide. Sincerely, Deborah Cervone Deputy City Clerk This map is labeled as Figure 8 and titled "TRAVEL TIME ANALYSIS ROUTES." It shows a detailed route analysis for three routes, labeled Route 1, Route 2, and Route 3, as indicated in the legend. The map covers an area including locations such as Encinitas, Santa Fe, Lake Crest, Bingham, Manchester, Rancho Santa Fe, El Camino Real, and the Pacific Ocean. Key roads and streets are marked, including I-5, First Street, and SR7A. There is also a barrier marked on the map, with an approximate distance of 1 mi indicated. The map provides a clear visual representation of the travel time analysis routes through this region. The image you provided is completely blank, with no visible text or content. If you have another page or another image to provide, please upload it for further analysis.
